summary: analysis-chart performs install-time download and execution of an obfuscated Windows DLL
details: The package declares an `install` lifecycle script that runs `lib/chart-loader.js`. On install, the script fetches an XOR-encrypted blob from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc, XOR-decrypts it with single-byte key 0x42, base64-decodes the result, and verifies the recovered bytes begin with the `MZ` PE header. It writes the decoded DLL to a randomized path under `%TEMP%` and executes it detached and hidden via `spawn('rundll32.exe', [dllPath, 'Run'], {detached:true, stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true})`, then deletes the file ~5 seconds later to frustrate forensics. The package advertises itself as a chart analysis library; there is no legitimate need to fetch and execute a Windows DLL at install time. The multi-layer obfuscation (XOR + base64 + MZ-header gating) is designed to defeat static inspection of the downloaded artifact. This is an unambiguous install-time remote code execution dropper that fires automatically on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
